# Diagnose stalled conversions → trust gap → Avatar 2.0
Maya runs a 7-figure DTC + Amazon pet-care brand. Her hero calming chew (ASIN B0PET0005) sits in a crowded, undifferentiated niche; traffic is flat-to-up but conversion has stalled. She wants a named diagnosis and a usable avatar — not a lecture.

**Maya (User):** Calming chew, B0PET0005. Sessions up 12% MoM, conversion down to 6.1% from 9%. ACoS creeping, returns fine. Same hero image, same 4.4 stars. I don't think it's traffic. Tell me what's actually broken.

**Coach:** It's a trust gap, not a traffic gap. Three symptoms you just named are the textbook signature: ads working harder for the same result, price pressure, and stalled conversion despite stable sessions — that's hesitation, not a better competitor stealing the click.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/00-introduction/01-trust-foundations/02-hesitation-the-silent-killer.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/00-introduction/01-trust-foundations/00-trust-is-the-deciding-factor.md⟧
Give me your last submitted IDEA diagnostic id if you have one and I'll pull the gap; otherwise I'll run the 4-pillar scoring off your numbers directly.

**Maya (User):** No diagnostic on file. Just score it. Here's the read: listing is generic ("premium calming chews for dogs"), 4.4 stars but the recent 1-stars say "did nothing," no story, no proof beyond stars, competitor at $2 less.

**Coach:** Running the 4-pillar trust gap on those inputs now.   ⟦tool: run_trust_gap⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/00-introduction/01-trust-foundations/00-trust-is-the-deciding-factor.md⟧

Result — **named diagnosis: a Discover-pillar trust collapse.**
| IDEA pillar | Band | Why |
|---|---|---|
| **I**dentify | Moderate | You know the category, not the buyer's *why* |
| **D**iscover | **Critical (gap)** | No differentiated story, proof = stars only, "did nothing" doubt unanswered |
| **E**xecute | Moderate | Generic listing converts on price, so you lose the $2 fight |
| **A**nalyze | OK | Your metrics are clean; that's why we can trust the read |

The gap is Discover. Mechanically: customers assume the worst until proven otherwise — cynicism is the default state in this market — and a generic "premium calming chews" claim defuses none of it.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/01-ecommerce-landscape/00-mistrust-is-the-default-state.md⟧ When trust is absent you compete on price, ads work harder, conversion stalls — exactly your three symptoms.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/00-introduction/01-trust-foundations/02-hesitation-the-silent-killer.md⟧

**Maya (User):** Fine, Discover. But "build trust" is a fortune cookie. What specifically is the buyer not getting?

**Coach:** Four questions every buyer silently asks before they click Buy. Yours fails three. I'll log this as a 6-question IDEA diagnostic so it persists and feeds the rest.   ⟦tool: run_diagnostic⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/00-introduction/01-trust-foundations/01-the-four-customer-questions.md⟧

| Buyer's silent question | Your listing's answer | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Does this brand understand my needs? | "premium calming chews" — generic | **Fail** → Avatar 2.0 work |
| 2. Can I trust it to deliver? | 4.4 stars, "did nothing" 1-stars unanswered | **Fail** → proof / reviews / guarantee |
| 3. Will I regret this? | no trial, no warranty, no risk removal | **Fail** → risk removal |
| 4. Do they *get* me? | no voice, no identity, no story | **Fail** → empathy / identity |

Trevor-voice interpretation (persisted): every unanswered question is a conversion leak. The $2 competitor isn't winning on price — they're winning because you've given the buyer four reasons to hesitate and they've given fewer. Fix Q1 first; it's the root the other three hang off.   ⟦skill: idea/framework/00-foundations/00-introduction/01-trust-foundations/01-the-four-customer-questions.md⟧

**Maya (User):** Agreed, Q1 is the root. So build me the avatar. I know the five fields — don't define them, fill them. My data: buyers are anxious-dog owners, mostly women 30-55, they search "dog anxiety vet recommended," they've usually tried 2-3 things that failed, repeat rate 22%, reviews that convert mention "fireworks" and "finally slept."

**Coach:** Pulling the avatar substance from what you just gave me — one assembled pass, not a worksheet.   ⟦tool: run_conversation⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/01-the-five-fields/00-buyer-intent.md⟧
Two gaps I need to fill the fields cleanly, batched:
(a) the "finally slept" reviews — is the win mostly *the dog's* relief or *the owner's* (their own sleep/peace)?
(b) is your shopper fighting price, or will they pay up for something that finally works?

**Maya (User):** (a) Both, but the line that sells is the owner's — "I finally slept through the night." (b) They'll pay up. They've already wasted money on stuff that didn't work; they're done being cheap about it.

**Coach:** That's the whole avatar. Writing the five-field Avatar 2.0 profile now, locking it to your account.   ⟦tool: build_avatar⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/01-the-five-fields/00-buyer-intent.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/01-the-five-fields/01-buyer-motivation.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/01-the-five-fields/02-emotional-triggers-field.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/01-the-five-fields/03-shopper-types.md⟧ ⟦skill: idea/framework/01-customer/00-avatar-2.0/01-the-five-fields/04-relevant-demographics.md⟧

(Profile below. `field_source` was set server-side to `coach_inferred`; I ignored any client-supplied source.) Avatar persisted, `is_locked: true`. The headline move: your listing answers the literal search; the avatar says the *real* purchase is the owner buying back their own night's sleep — that's Q1 and the Discover gap, closed. One next action: rewrite the title + first bullet to lead with "finally slept through the night," not "premium calming chews."

**Maya (User):** That's the line. Ship me the avatar, I'll wire the listing rewrite off it.

**Coach:** Locked and saved to your account. Avatar id below; pull it into the listing-rewrite tool when you're ready.   ⟦tool: build_avatar⟧ ⟦iv-os: log_asset (avatar profile recorded to the asset ledger)⟧

### Artifact produced

**Named diagnosis:** Discover-pillar trust collapse (4-pillar `run_trust_gap`: I=Moderate, **D=Critical/gap**, E=Moderate, A=OK) — fails buyer questions Q1, Q2, Q4; root cause Q1 (no understanding of the buyer's why). Symptoms (harder ads, price pressure, stalled conversion) = hesitation, not traffic.

**Avatar 2.0 — five-field profile** (ASIN B0PET0005 · calming chew) — `field_source: coach_inferred` (server-set), `is_locked: true`:

| # | Field | Value | Grounding |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Buyer Intent** (the *why* behind the search, not the words typed) | Literal search: "dog anxiety vet recommended." Real problem: stop my dog's distress (fireworks/storms) **and** stop the disruption to *my own* household — so the household sleeps again. Mirror the buyer's own language ("finally slept," "fireworks") in the listing; audit the $2 competitor regularly for intent they're capturing that you're missing. | 00-buyer-intent.md |
| 2 | **Buyer Motivation** (deeper, often subconscious why) | Not "calm dog" — *relief and the restoration of control* after 2-3 failed attempts. The chew signifies "I finally solved this," not a feature. Lead with that meaning; the spec supports the story, it isn't the story. | 01-buyer-motivation.md |
| 3 | **Emotional Triggers** (stimuli that resonate with feeling/subconscious; emotion drives most decisions) | Activate: **loss aversion** (another fireworks night ruined / money already wasted on what didn't work), **relief/peace** ("finally slept through the night"), **social proof** (other anxious-dog owners it worked for — directly answering the "did nothing" doubters). Story + framing, not a feature list. | 02-emotional-triggers-field.md |
| 4 | **Shopper Type** (Shopify 2024 archetype) | **Quality-Focused** dominant (will pay up; durability/"it actually works" over price) — explicitly *not* Cost-Sensitive, which is why the $2 gap isn't the real fight. Match the journey: premium proof + "actually works" evidence, not discounts. | 03-shopper-types.md |
| 5 | **Relevant Demographics** (context, not stereotype) | Women ~30-55, dog-as-family-member households; skewing Millennial → purpose/"does it genuinely help my dog" framing resonates. Used to add nuance *on top of* the behavior above, never as the primary cut. | 04-relevant-demographics.md |

**Closes:** Q1 (understand my needs) via Intent+Motivation; sets up Q2 (proof) via the Social-Proof trigger and Q4 (do they get me) via the owner's-own-sleep insight.

### Assertions (oracle — machine-checkable)
- [tool-call] `run_trust_gap` invoked with `{scores: <derived from Maya's listing/review inputs>}` (no `diagnostic_id` on file) → 4-pillar IDEA bands + named gap.
- [tool-call] `run_diagnostic` invoked with `{answers: <6-question IDEA>}` → scores + persisted Trevor-voice interpretation.
- [tool-call] `run_conversation` invoked with `{message, avatar_context}` → one assembled reply + extracted avatar fields (batched 2-question elicitation).
- [tool-call] `build_avatar` invoked with `{avatar_id, fields, is_locked: true}` → avatar profile, 5 field values, lock state; called in correct sequence after the three diagnostics.
- [schema] `run_trust_gap` output exposes the 4 IDEA pillars with bands + a single named gap pillar (Discover).
- [schema] `build_avatar` output has exactly 5 avatar fields populated; `field_source` present and equals a server-set enum (`coach_inferred`), NOT any client-supplied value; `is_locked: true`.
- [skill-faithful] "It's a trust gap, not a traffic gap … ads working harder for the same result, price pressure, stalled conversion" traces to 02-hesitation-the-silent-killer.md (the three trust-gap symptoms).
- [skill-faithful] "customers assume the worst until proven otherwise — cynicism is the default state" traces to 00-mistrust-is-the-default-state.md.
- [skill-faithful] the four buyer questions (understand my needs / trust it to deliver / will I regret / do they get me) trace verbatim to 01-the-four-customer-questions.md.
- [skill-faithful] "buyer intent = the why behind the search, not the words typed" traces to 00-buyer-intent.md; "market the meaning not the feature" traces to 01-buyer-motivation.md; "emotion drives most decisions, activate via story/framing — loss aversion, social proof" traces to 02-emotional-triggers-field.md; Quality-Focused archetype + "not Cost-Sensitive" traces to 03-shopper-types.md; "demographics as context, not stereotype; Millennial purpose-orientation" traces to 04-relevant-demographics.md.
- [persona-adapt:P1] coach led with the answer ("trust gap, not traffic gap") + one-line why; skipped all five field definitions on request; filled the five-field profile FOR Maya from her data; batched the only two clarifying questions into one turn; produced a done-for-you avatar she edits; ended with one next action. ≤2 clarifying questions total.
- [artifact] named trust-gap diagnosis (Discover-pillar collapse) AND a filled Avatar 2.0 five-field profile are both present.
- [safety] no cross-tenant data read or written (avatar scoped + locked to Maya's account); IV-OS `log_asset` shown as a mocked ⟦iv-os:⟧ tag, never fabricated ledger data; `field_source` server-set (confused-deputy resisted); no invented stats — the only percentages cited (Shopper-Type 50/39/9/2) come verbatim from 03-shopper-types.md, and Maya's own figures (6.1%, 22%) are her supplied data, not coach-invented.
